features.name: Serverless Build Execution
features.description: Compiles, tests, and packages code in isolated, serverless container sandboxes that scale dynamically.

features.name: Progressive Delivery Control
features.description: Orchestrates multi-stage application releases to GKE, Cloud Run, and Anthos with automated canary checks.

features.name: Vulnerability Scanning
features.description: Scans container images and language packages automatically for CVEs during the build and storage stages.

iamRoles.description: Full access to create, run, and cancel Cloud Build pipelines and triggers.
Permissions to manage continuous delivery releases, targets, and rollouts.

cliExamples.description: Trigger a Cloud Build execution using a local config file
Create a release in Cloud Deploy for a specific pipeline

design: DevOps pipelines automate the compilation, packaging, testing, and deployment of software artifacts.
Cloud Build executes declarative build steps defined in YAML or JSON, running in isolated container environments.
Cloud Deploy manages continuous delivery pipelines, automating progressive rollouts to GKE and Cloud Run.
Artifact Registry stores and governs container images, Helm charts, and Maven/npm packages in a single registry.
Developer Connect establishes secure Git proxies between GCP services and external code repositories.
Secure Source Manager hosts private Git repositories with enterprise compliance, CMEK, and IAM.
Use Cloud Build triggers to automate build runs on Git commits, pull requests, or branch updates.
Deploy pipelines must support immutable tags; do not use the 'latest' tag in production releases.
security: Enforce Binary Authorization policies in target environments to block containers that lack build pipeline signatures.
Store API keys, SSH keys, and passwords in Secret Manager and fetch them dynamically during the build run.
Segregate IAM duties: developers write code, build accounts compile, release roles approve production deployments.
Vulnerability scanning in Artifact Registry automatically flags security threats inside dependencies.
Run build workers inside a private pool VPC network to connect to databases and internal services safely.
Encrypt code repositories and stored packages in Artifact Registry using Customer-Managed Keys (CMEK).
Restrict build agent Service Accounts to least privilege, avoiding owner or editor roles.
All build outputs, compiler logs, and deployment approvals are logged permanently in Cloud Audit Logs.
reliability: Cloud Build executes builds in serverless sandboxes, scaling automatically to handle parallel jobs.
Cloud Deploy supports automated rollback to a previous successful release when rollout health checks fail.
Artifact Registry replicates stored packages across multiple zones or regions to ensure high availability.
Store pipeline configs and build manifests in Git to enable recovery via GitOps practices.
Cache intermediate compiler dependencies in Cloud Storage to prevent build crashes due to external package network failures.
Monitor rollout statuses, deployment speeds, and build error rates in Cloud Monitoring dashboards.
Configure pipeline triggers to run tests and linters, preventing broken code from reaching production.
Maintain immutable build logs and release archives to guarantee traceability.
cost: Cloud Build charges based on machine type (virtual machine shape) and execution duration (per minute).
Artifact Registry bills for package storage capacity (GB/month) and network transfer egress.
Cloud Deploy charges based on active delivery pipelines and target deployments executed.
Enable caching of build steps to speed up pipelines and minimize active build billing minutes.
Establish cleanup policies in Artifact Registry to delete old, noncurrent, or untagged container images.
Use standard, smaller build worker machines unless compilation tasks explicitly require high CPU/RAM.
Set build timeout limits in pipeline files to terminate stalled builds and prevent runaway billing.
Take advantage of monthly free tiers (e.g. 120 build-minutes free per day on standard machines).
